title: Why I Love Cardboard Standees!
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=6iCAxQUz4aQ

Daniel (@dungeondive) makes an impassioned case for cardboard standees over plastic miniatures, touching on plastic responsibility, ready-to-use convenience, and the simple joy of vibrant 2D art that doesn't require painting skill or time investment.

He examines the Wanderer's Emporium DM Starter Pack from Amazon (183 pieces for around $50), whose hard-pressed cardboard and front-and-back art impress despite somewhat cartoony styling. The Pathfinder Pawns get extensive coverage—Daniel has organised his collection into faction groups using jewellery cases from the sadly departed Joann's. League of Dungeoneers standees earn particular praise for their exceptional quality and consistent art style.

The real revelation is the stand holder comparison. Those clear plastic clips? They damage cardboard and obscure small minis. The Legends of Andor-style clips from Etsy? Gentle on cardboard and available in colours for faction differentiation. The Dungeon Crusade holders Roger Dearing sent him? Even better, though their source remains mysterious. Daniel's plea to Cool Mini or Not: embrace the "or not" in your name.

----

*Team cardboard or team plastic—and what's your preferred stand holder solution?*
